content.name: EXXX: Human Development and Learning Science

c-nHeKFgF3C5.content: This course gives a survey-level focus on how educators create supportive learning environments by modeling skills through intentional design and evaluation. It emphasizes the application of evidence-based strategies to motivate students, including careful vetting of instructional technologies. This course also explores developmental strategies that support students’ social-emotional growth, while connecting theories of learning and human development to modern classroom practice, including key principles from the Science of Reading.

content.name: EXXX: Principles and Analysis of Curriculum

c-nHeKFgF3C5.content: This course focuses on evaluating curriculum through the application of established theories, principles, and models, and using that analysis to inform continuous improvement. Learners develop curriculum maps that build on existing skills, prior learning, and historical data, while aligning curriculum to the evolving needs of students through the integration of EdTech and blended teaching models. The course also explores curriculum recommendations that respond to changing standards and demands, ensuring curriculum is accessible to all learners.

This course walks through the evaluation instructional models, theories, and evidence-based approaches that support effective teaching. Learners apply these practices to differentiate instruction, including the integration of EdTech to meet diverse student needs. The course introduces implementation strategies for communicating meaningful feedback, incorporating multi-disciplinary approaches into instruction, and applying evidence-based methods to strengthen student understanding and long-term knowledge retention.

This course focuses on foundational understanding of assessment principles and forms of assessment. It introduces the process for designing and implementing assessments, analyzing assessment validity and risk, and using assessment data. The course explores concepts of assessment enhancement and refinement based on data-driven insights, with analysis and implementation of technology with assessment.

content.name: EXXX: Professional and Ethical Use of Educational Technology

c-nHeKFgF3C5.content: This course focuses on the integration of educational technology across curriculum, assessment, and instruction to enhance student learning. The course explains the process of evaluating and recommending appropriate technologies, including assistive and accessibility tools, to support diverse learner needs. The course also touches on analyzing the technical aspects, ethical use, potential risks, and mitigation strategies associated with emerging technologies, including AI, to ensure responsible and effective implementation in educational settings.

This course focuses on research literacy by building foundational knowledge of research principles, methodologies, and processes in education. The course explains key aspects of research, including: qualitative and quantitative approaches, finding resources, research questions, and problem statements. The course also builds out foundations for research analysis, including: data collection methods, reading, interpreting, and analyzing research studies, and data collection instruments. Through these foundational steps, learners are prepared to engage in tasks such as developing annotated bibliographies, conducting literature reviews, and selecting research and data analysis methods.

content.name: EXXX: Collaboration and Consultation in Education

c-nHeKFgF3C5.content: This course focuses on instructional leadership with an emphasis on curriculum development and implementation across educational settings. It explores the application of leadership strategies to support effective instruction through collaboration with teacher groups, departments, school boards, families, communities, and other stakeholders. The course also dives into the use of data-informed decision-making for curriculum alignment, professional development planning, and teaching practices and instruction.

This course focuses on developing core leadership strategies that support effective teaching and learning. The course examines curriculum design and alignment, along with foundational principles of assessment for learning, including how to analyze and use data to guide instructional decisions. The course emphasizes applying common instructional leadership practices to improve curriculum implementation, monitor student progress, and drive data-informed instruction that enhances student outcomes.
This course centers on equity, access, and culturally responsive professional practice in literacy development.
The Master of Science in Curriculum and Instruction Capstone is the culminating course of the degree. It unites content area knowledge with the completion of a research project or study.
